title: Q: Strange verilator behaviour when running from a script
body:
I normally run verilator and then execute the output file from a script. Today something really odd happened. When the sim is run within the script it finishes ahead of time, after a number of clock cycles. It looks like the module eval() function is exiting the program with an exit code of 141 (decimal).

But if I call the same executable from the bash shell directly, the simulation completes to the end and the exit code is zero.

I have tried dumping the environment variables and reading and exporting them from the shell before calling the verilator sim. That works ok too: the sim runs all the way to the end.

I do not see what is different for the simulation depending on whether it is called from the script or bash, as I have the same arguments and environment variables. But I get that exit code of 141 consistently.

Could you give me some hints to debug this? I don't know what to check next.

PS: I am running this on MacOS, in case it matters.
